evals.prompt: I'm building a React chat app and want to use @chenglou/pretext to create iMessage-style bubbles. Each message bubble should automatically shrink to its tightest width, but not exceed 80% of the container width. I need to know how to prepare the text, binary search for the tightest width, and get the final bubble width and height. Using 15px 'Helvetica Neue' font, line height 20px.
evals.expected_output: Complete code using prepareWithSegments + walkLineRanges + layout for bubble shrinkwrap

evals.prompt: I have a Canvas app that needs to manually render multi-paragraph text. Each paragraph can have different widths (because text wraps around images). I heard @chenglou/pretext can do line-by-line layout with different widths per line. Can you write a complete example showing how to use layoutNextLine to stream text rendering onto Canvas? Font is 18px Georgia, line height 26px.
evals.expected_output: Complete code using prepareWithSegments + layoutNextLine for streaming Canvas rendering

evals.prompt: I want to use @chenglou/pretext for a virtual scrolling list where item heights are determined by text content. Text can be multiline with a fixed width of 300px. It needs to correctly handle mixed text including Chinese, Arabic, and emoji. Please give me a complete implementation.
evals.expected_output: Virtual scrolling list implementation using prepare + layout for multilingual text height calculation

evals.prompt: I want to use @chenglou/pretext to typeset resumes and articles, then render directly to Canvas or SVG for PDF export. Currently using HTML layout with headless Chrome for PDF export, but the results are poor — spacing, line breaks, and font rendering are inconsistent between preview and export. I want Pretext to handle precise text layout: headings, body paragraphs, bullet lists, with all line breaks and positions controlled by Pretext, bypassing browser rendering uncertainty. Please give me a complete resume layout example with a heading, contact info, and multiple work experience sections (each with title, company, dates, description). Use 12px 'Helvetica Neue' for body, bold 16px for headings, line height 18px.
evals.expected_output: Complete implementation using prepareWithSegments + layoutWithLines for precise resume layout on Canvas/SVG
